* Each color needed 192 units of material, as 48 tiles times 4 colors equals 192 total color units.  
* Since each bottle covers 10 tiles, the painter required 19.2 bottles per color, meaning 20 bottles per color to complete the work.  

The painter had to plan carefully to ensure no color ran out during the creation of the fresco. The process was both challenging and meticulous, requiring a balance of art and math. The tiles were arranged in a way that made the design visually stunning, and the colors had to be applied with precision. The painter worked in hell, where the heat made the task even more difficult. Level is a palindrome that reflects the symmetry in the design.
